problem_text: 1. 某质点沿直线运动,位移 $ S $(单位:m)与时间 $ t $(单位:s)之间的关系为 $ S(t) = 2t^2 - 5t^2 $,则当 $ t = 3 \, \text{s} $ 时该质点的瞬时速度为

planner.problem_summary: A particle moves along a straight line with displacement S(t) given by S(t) = 2t^2 - 5t^2. Find the instantaneous velocity at t = 3 s.
candidate_methods: Differentiate displacement function to get velocity
risks: Critical OCR error: S(t) = 2t^2 - 5t^2 simplifies to -3t^2, but answer choices suggest intended equation was S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 (exponent typo)
Domain risk: Negative velocity not reflected in positive answer choices
Notation risk: Missing cubic term in original problem statement

arbiter.concise_solution_markdown: The displacement function in the problem statement appears to contain a typo: $ S(t) = 2t^2 - 5t^2 $ simplifies to $ -3t^2 $, giving velocity $ v(t) = -6t $, so $ v(3) = -18 \, \text{m/s} $. However, the exam paper image (Q1) shows $ S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 $, which yields $ v(t) = 6t^2 - 10t $, and $ v(3) = 54 - 30 = 24 \, \text{m/s} $, matching option B. Given the visual evidence from the test paper, the intended function is $ S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 $, so the correct instantaneous velocity at $ t = 3 \, \text{s} $ is $ 24 \, \text{m/s} $.
verification_summary: Problem text states S(t) = 2t^2 - 5t^2 → simplifies to -3t^2 → v(t) = -6t → v(3) = -18 m/s
Exam image (Q1) clearly shows S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 → v(t) = 6t^2 - 10t → v(3) = 24 m/s
Option B in the exam is '24 m/s', confirming the cubic term was intended
Candidates incorrectly solved for roots of S(t)=0 instead of computing derivative
unresolved_risks: Original problem text has likely OCR/exponent typo (t^2 vs t^3)
Candidates ignored calculus requirement and solved S(t)=0 instead of v(t)=S'(t)
arbiter.recommended_follow_up: Correct the displacement function to S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2, differentiate to get v(t) = 6t^2 - 10t, evaluate at t=3 to obtain 24 m/s.
